assets.name: User Credentials (email + password hash)
assets.description: User account email addresses and MD5-hashed passwords stored in SQLite Users table. MD5 hashing makes these trivially reversible. Used for authentication.

assets.name: JWT RSA Private Key
assets.description: RSA private key used to sign all JWT tokens, hardcoded in lib/insecurity.ts:26. Any party with access to source code can sign arbitrary JWTs as any user including admin.

assets.name: User Personal Data (PII)
assets.description: User profile data including full name, address, credit card numbers, mobile numbers stored across multiple Sequelize models (Address, Card, User).

assets.name: Payment Card Data
assets.description: Credit card numbers (CardNum), expiry months/years stored in Card model without encryption. Includes test cards like 4716190207394368.

assets.name: Confidential Business Documents
assets.description: M&A acquisition plans in /ftp/acquisitions.md, incident support KeePass database at /ftp/incident-support.kdbx, and coupon codes in /ftp/coupons_2013.md.bak. All publicly accessible via directory listing.

assets.name: Application Source Code and Config
assets.description: Node.js TypeScript source including security library (lib/insecurity.ts with hardcoded private key, HMAC secret). Config files exposing Google OAuth client ID and server configuration.

assets.name: SQLite Database File
assets.description: data/juiceshop.sqlite contains all user data, orders, and challenge state. The challenge tracking table reveals which challenges each user has solved.

assets.name: Access Logs
assets.description: HTTP access logs stored in logs/access.log.* and accessible via /support/logs directory listing. Reveals user activity patterns, IP addresses, and browsed URLs.

assets.name: Product Catalog and Pricing
assets.description: Product names, descriptions, prices, and quantities stored in SQLite Products table. Modifiable by authenticated users due to intentionally commented-out authorization.

assets.name: TOTP Secrets (2FA Seeds)
assets.description: TOTP secret seeds stored in Users.totpSecret field in SQLite. These are the 2FA enrollment keys that, if extracted, allow bypassing 2FA.

assets.name: Application Availability
assets.description: The running Juice Shop instance serving training content. DoS via RCE, XML billion-laughs, ZIP bomb, or NoSQL sleep injection can make it unavailable.

assets.name: User Security Answers
assets.description: Answers to security questions stored in SecurityAnswer model, used for password reset flow. Predictable answers enable account takeover without knowing password.
